steps.title: Identify what the figurative language must connect
steps.content: The claim specifies that Toomer uses figurative language to connect two distinct spaces: the narrator's urban environment of Washington, DC, and the rural South of the past. The right quotation must explicitly reference both places and link them through figurative language.

steps.title: Match the figurative bridge
steps.content: Choice B uses the simile “falls like a fertile shower” to show soil from “my homeland” (the rural South) falling upon “the lean streets” of Washington, DC. The other three describe single environments, lack figurative language connecting the places, or have no geographic reference. That matches choice B—B is correct.
